basics.name: Tianhao (Andy) Qin
basics.label: Robotics Engineer
basics.summary: An enthusiastic roboticist and explorer of the world.

education.institution: University of Michigan, College of Engineering
courses: ROB599 - Deep Learning for Robot Perception

education.institution: New York University, Tandon School of Engineering
courses: Minor in Computer Science and Robotics

work.name: NYU Robotic Design Team, Vertically Integrated Projects
work.summary: Lead to compete in NASA Robotic Mining Competition: Lunabotics
highlights: Led a team of 50 to design and manufacture a rover that aimed to excavate and deposit icy regolith below surface on the Moon.
Implemented Agile project management according to NASA's systems engineering standards throughout project life cycle.
Developed ConOp, System Requirements, and subsequent Verification Plans following the “V” model of systems engineering.

work.name: CSE Department, New York University
highlights: Graded Python assignments with comments and held office hours for 10 hours weekly.
Taught 3-hour lab sections on Python and reviewed professor's lecture materials every Friday.

work.name: AI4CE Lab, New York University
work.summary: Continued working on cool projects after doing summer research.
highlights: Revised pose estimation algorithm to project water pipeline information onto planar surface.
Tested real-time SLAM with projector on a Unitree A1 robot dog.
Researched decentralized formation control algorithms and simulated soft particle movements in Pybullet.
Published an abstract during the 2021 NYU UGSRP Showcase.

work.name: Future Labs, New York University
work.summary: Click link above to see me holding a mic
highlights: Produced skateboarding wheels with biodegradable polyurethanes and researched optimal plastic combinations.
Designed 3D wheel injection molds with SOLIWORKS and simulated mold fabrication through CAM in Fusion 360.
Manufactured molds from polyethylene plastic blocks through OtherMill CNC machine.

awards.summary: The fund is only $1500 though, but enough for a wild prototype.
